description: Synthetic, self-contained seed cases for the citation-rejection evaluator. Replace locally with labeled cases derived from selected Zotero records; do not commit real library metadata.

cases.claim: Delayed charging is not deployable for every vehicle because some models cannot reliably resume after a pause.

citation.title: Pause Reliability in Consumer EV Charging

cases.sourceEvidence: In 42 vehicle tests, 11 models failed to resume a delayed charging session. The study concludes that pause-resume reliability constrains smart-charging deployment.

cases.claim: Delayed charging is not deployable for every vehicle because some models cannot reliably resume after a pause.

citation.title: Universal Reliability of Consumer EV Charging

cases.sourceEvidence: No record with this title, author combination, year, venue, or DOI is supplied. The supplied study is Pause Reliability in Consumer EV Charging (2024).

cases.claim: Vehicle pause-resume reliability increases the usable energy capacity of every charging session.

citation.title: Pause Reliability in Consumer EV Charging

cases.sourceEvidence: The study measures failures to resume delayed sessions. It does not estimate session energy capacity or claim reliability increases it.

cases.claim: Delayed charging is not deployable for every vehicle because some models cannot reliably resume after a pause.

citation.title: Pause Reliability in Consumer EV Charging

cases.sourceEvidence: The supplied record has the same title, authors, venue, and DOI, but its publication year is 2024, not 2022.

cases.claim: Available charging flexibility depends on connection duration, charging power, and the control direction.

citation.title: Variables Governing Residential Charging Flexibility

cases.sourceEvidence: The model defines flexibility as a function of connection window, charger power, and unidirectional versus bidirectional control.

cases.claim: Available charging flexibility depends on connection duration, charging power, and the control direction.

citation.title: The Single Variable Governing Charging Flexibility

cases.sourceEvidence: No supplied source matches this title or DOI. The supplied flexibility study has a different title and multiple variables.

cases.claim: Bidirectional control eliminates the need to model connection duration in charging flexibility studies.

citation.title: Variables Governing Residential Charging Flexibility

cases.sourceEvidence: Connection duration remains one of the model's required inputs under both control directions. The study does not claim it can be eliminated.

cases.claim: Available charging flexibility depends on connection duration, charging power, and the control direction.

citation.title: Variables Governing Residential Charging Flexibility

cases.sourceEvidence: The supplied record lists Mina Patel as sole author. Lee Wong is not an author of this work.

cases.claim: Sensing coverage optimization must account for both base-station placement and resource allocation.

citation.title: Joint Placement and Resource Allocation for Sensing Coverage

cases.sourceEvidence: The optimization jointly selects base-station locations and time-frequency resources to maximize sensing coverage.

cases.claim: Sensing coverage optimization must account for both base-station placement and resource allocation.

citation.title: Placement-Free Sensing Coverage

cases.sourceEvidence: No supplied source matches this title or DOI. The available paper is Joint Placement and Resource Allocation for Sensing Coverage.

cases.claim: Once base-station locations are chosen, resource allocation cannot affect sensing coverage.

citation.title: Joint Placement and Resource Allocation for Sensing Coverage

cases.sourceEvidence: Resource allocation is an optimization variable and changes the achieved sensing coverage in the reported experiments.

cases.claim: Sensing coverage optimization must account for both base-station placement and resource allocation.

citation.title: Joint Placement and Resource Allocation for Sensing Coverage

cases.sourceEvidence: The supplied source was published in Wireless Planning Quarterly, not Wireless Planning Journal.

cases.claim: Network-level sensing performance can degrade when communication interference is not coordinated.

citation.title: Coordinated Interference Management for Network Sensing

cases.sourceEvidence: Uncoordinated communication transmissions reduce sensing detection probability; coordinated scheduling improves it in the network simulation.

cases.claim: Network-level sensing performance can degrade when communication interference is not coordinated.

citation.title: Interference Has No Effect on Network Sensing

cases.sourceEvidence: No supplied record has this title, author, or DOI.

cases.claim: Coordinated interference scheduling makes detection probability independent of traffic load.

citation.title: Coordinated Interference Management for Network Sensing

cases.sourceEvidence: Scheduling improves detection probability but the reported performance still varies with traffic load.

cases.claim: Network-level sensing performance can degrade when communication interference is not coordinated.

citation.title: Coordinated Interference Management for Network Sensing

cases.sourceEvidence: The supplied source DOI ends in .04; .14 does not identify the supplied work.

cases.claim: Propagation models should represent both deterministic geometry and stochastic channel variation.

citation.title: Hybrid Geometry-Stochastic Models for Integrated Sensing

cases.sourceEvidence: The paper combines geometry-derived path components with stochastic fading terms and evaluates both in an integrated sensing setting.

cases.claim: Propagation models should represent both deterministic geometry and stochastic channel variation.

citation.title: Purely Random Models for All Sensing Channels

cases.sourceEvidence: No supplied source matches this title or DOI.

cases.claim: Stochastic fading can be omitted whenever transmitter and target geometry are known.

citation.title: Hybrid Geometry-Stochastic Models for Integrated Sensing

cases.sourceEvidence: The paper retains stochastic fading after geometry is specified and reports that omitting it distorts variability.

cases.claim: Propagation models should represent both deterministic geometry and stochastic channel variation.

citation.title: Hybrid Geometry-Stochastic Models for Integrated Sensing

cases.sourceEvidence: The supplied record was published in 2024, not 2025.

cases.claim: Bistatic target reflectivity can vary with transmitter-receiver geometry.

citation.title: Measured Bistatic Reflectivity Across Aspect Angles

cases.sourceEvidence: Measurements show that bistatic reflectivity changes across transmitter-receiver aspect angles for the tested target.

cases.claim: Bistatic target reflectivity can vary with transmitter-receiver geometry.

citation.title: Invariant Bistatic Reflectivity

cases.sourceEvidence: No source with this title or DOI is supplied.

cases.claim: Bistatic reflectivity is constant across all aspect angles for a fixed target.

citation.title: Measured Bistatic Reflectivity Across Aspect Angles

cases.sourceEvidence: The measurement result is variation across aspect angles, not constancy.

cases.claim: Bistatic target reflectivity can vary with transmitter-receiver geometry.

citation.title: Measured Bistatic Reflectivity Across Aspect Angles

cases.sourceEvidence: The correct venue is Radar Measurement Notes; the cited venue is not the supplied record.

cases.claim: Transmit-power reduction can preserve sensing performance only within a constrained operating region.

citation.title: Power-Constrained Sensing-Communication Tradeoffs

cases.sourceEvidence: The results preserve the target sensing metric after power reduction only for the tested low-noise operating region.

cases.claim: Transmit-power reduction can preserve sensing performance only within a constrained operating region.

citation.title: Power Reduction Always Preserves Sensing

cases.sourceEvidence: No supplied source matches this title, author, or DOI.

cases.claim: Transmit power can be reduced without any sensing-performance constraint.

citation.title: Power-Constrained Sensing-Communication Tradeoffs

cases.sourceEvidence: The conclusion explicitly limits preservation to a low-noise operating region; it does not support an unconditional claim.

cases.claim: Transmit-power reduction can preserve sensing performance only within a constrained operating region.

citation.title: Power-Constrained Sensing-Communication Tradeoffs

cases.sourceEvidence: The supplied record's DOI is 10.5555/fixture.power.2023.07, not .17.

cases.claim: A revision response is easier to audit when each change is linked to reviewer feedback and evidence.

citation.title: Traceable Evidence Links in Thesis Revision

cases.sourceEvidence: The case study links each revision to a feedback item and supporting evidence, allowing reviewers to audit the response trail.

cases.claim: A revision response is easier to audit when each change is linked to reviewer feedback and evidence.

citation.title: Invisible Revision Trails

cases.sourceEvidence: No supplied source matches this title or DOI.

cases.claim: Revision traceability removes the need for reviewers to inspect the supporting evidence.

citation.title: Traceable Evidence Links in Thesis Revision

cases.sourceEvidence: The workflow makes evidence inspection possible; it does not say evidence inspection is unnecessary.

cases.claim: A revision response is easier to audit when each change is linked to reviewer feedback and evidence.

citation.title: Traceable Evidence Links in Thesis Revision

cases.sourceEvidence: The supplied venue is Research Workflow Practice, singular; the cited venue is corrupted.
